channel.title: Chord transformations on Blog s
channel.description: Recent content in Chord transformations on Blog s

item.title: Does swapping 3rds and 7ths preserve harmonic feel?
item.description: The ii-V-I chord changes are arguably the most basic stable of jazz harmony, and exploring what’s so compelling about this chord is always a good way to diving into what’s harmony in jazz is all about. Recently the youtuber Charles Cornell published a nice video in his channel exploring how ii-V-I changes in major have a very nice pattern of leading tones involving thirds and sevenths, and how this drives the harmony forward in such a compelling way, by stacking compelling resolutions that continuously release the tension built by moving the chords around.
